title: Olfersia tecta
taxonomy: Olfersia tecta is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The genus Olfersia is a group of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions, characterized by their divided leaves and often epiphytic habits. Further classification would place Olfersia tecta within the order Polypodiales, a diverse group of ferns with a wide range of growth forms. This classification reflects the complex and still-evolving relationships between different plant groups.

intro: Olfersia tecta, also known as Olfersia, is a type of plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Olfersia tecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Olfersia tecta is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory infections, coughs, and colds.
